title: helianthemum polifolium subsp. pilosum
intro: Helianthemum polifolium subsp. pilosum, also known as rockrose, is a flowering plant species within the family Cistaceae, native to Europe and Asia. Traditionally, rockrose has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its essential oil and flavonoids. The health benefits of rockrose include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and act as an antiseptic to promote wound healing. Rockrose can be prepared as an herbal tea, tincture, or topical ointment, and is often used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including skin conditions and respiratory issues.
